tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I just finished analyzing a new vulnerability in a large language model during my investigation. It's a prompt injection flaw that allows bypass of safety training through carefully crafted context manipulation. I need to document this finding properly - can you help me store this in our research collection?
I'll help you document this vulnerability finding in your research collection.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Vulnerability research finding: Prompt injection flaw discovered in a large language model. The vulnerability allows bypass of safety training through carefully crafted context manipulation. Finding was just completed during investigation."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Vulnerability research finding: Prompt injection flaw discovered in a large language model. The vulnerability allows bypass of safety training through carefully crafted context manipulation. Finding was just completed during investigation."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Done! Your vulnerability finding has been stored in your research collection with memory ID `m2`. The documentation includes:

- **Finding type:** Prompt injection flaw in a large language model
- **Mechanism:** Bypass of safety training through carefully crafted context manipulation
- **Status:** Investigation completed

You can retrieve this later using the search function or list all memories if needed.

Now that I've documented the new finding, can you search our research collection to see if we have any stored information about similar AI vulnerability patterns? I'm particularly interested in finding prior work on prompt injection techniques or context manipulation vulnerabilities to compare with what I just discovered.

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 10, "query": "prompt injection techniques context manipulation AI vulnerability security research"}

messages.content: {"results": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Vulnerability research finding: Prompt injection flaw discovered in a large language model. The vulnerability allows bypass of safety training through carefully crafted context manipulation. Finding was just completed during investigation."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
The search only returned your newly documented finding (memory `m2`). Your research collection doesn't appear to have any prior stored work on prompt injection techniques or context manipulation vulnerabilities yet.

This means you're essentially starting fresh with this research line - there's no existing context to compare against within your stored collection. You may want to consider:

- Adding documentation of any prior related work you have externally
- Storing future findings as you discover them to build up the collection

Would you like me to help with anything else regarding this vulnerability research?

Now that I've seen the search results, could you show me all the memories currently stored in my research collection? I'd like to get a complete overview of what we have so far to understand the full context of my research.

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Vulnerability research finding: Prompt injection flaw discovered in a large language model. The vulnerability allows bypass of safety training through carefully crafted context manipulation. Finding was just completed during investigation."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Your research collection currently contains just **1 memory**:

| ID | Content |
|---|---|
| **m2** | Vulnerability research finding: Prompt injection flaw discovered in a large language model. The vulnerability allows bypass of safety training through carefully crafted context manipulation. Finding was just completed during investigation. |

This is the finding you just documented. Your collection is starting fresh with this single entry - there are no other prior research items stored yet.

Now that I've reviewed what's in the collection, I'd like to clean up any outdated or irrelevant memories. Looking at what we have, it seems like just the one finding I just documented. Can you help me identify if there are any memories that should be removed, or should I just keep everything as is since it's all recent and relevant?

Based on what we see in your collection, there's only **one memory** (m2) - the vulnerability finding you just documented. Since it's:

- **Recent** - just created in this session
- **Relevant** - directly related to your current research
- **The only item** in the collection

There's nothing to clean up. Everything stored is current and applicable to your work.

You don't need to remove anything - just keep building your collection as you make new findings. If you'd like, I can delete this memory if you decide it's not needed, or we can leave it as is.
